case.title: FERRANTE v. CITY OF NEW YORK

opinion.h: FERRANTE v. CITY OF NEW YORK, 385 U.S. 9 (1966)
FERRANTE v. CITY OF NEW YORK ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 277. Decided October 10, 1966.
opinion.p: The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
385 U.S. 9 UNITED STATES v. OHIO. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE SIXTH CIRCUIT. No. 271. Decided October 10, 1966.
Solicitor General Marshall, Assistant Attorney General Douglas, Morton Hollander and J. F. Bishop for the United States.
William B. Saxbe, Attorney General of Ohio, and Gerald A. Donahue, First Assistant Attorney General, for respondent.
The petition for a writ of certiorari is granted and the judgment is reversed. Wickard v. Filburn, 317 U.S. 111.
